title: Overnight Chia Pudding
description: No-cook make-ahead breakfast or snack with chia, plant milk, and fruit. Ready when you wake up.
explanation: Chia seeds gel in liquid overnight into a tapioca-like pudding. It is ideal for busy mornings, travel prep, and anyone who wants breakfast without cooking.
Soy milk raises protein; oat or almond milk keeps it lighter. Sweeten with fruit or a little jaggery rather than lots of sugar.
ingredients: 1 tsp Maple Syrup or Jaggery powder (optional)
1 tbsp Nuts or Seeds for topping
instructions: In a jar, whisk plant milk, chia seeds, sweetener, and vanilla or cardamom.
Wait 5 minutes, stir again so seeds do not clump at the bottom.
Cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
In the morning, stir, add fruit and nuts, and eat cold—or warm gently if preferred.
stepNotes: A second stir after 5 minutes is the difference between smooth pudding and seed clumps.
If too thick in the morning, loosen with a splash of plant milk.
Use a jar with a lid for grab-and-go mornings.
Cardamom gives an Indian dessert feel; cocoa powder makes a chocolate version.

variationSections.title: Flavor ideas
items: Cocoa peanut with cocoa powder and a swirl of peanut butter.
Apple cinnamon with grated apple and cinnamon.

variationSections.title: Protein upgrades
items: Use fortified soy milk as the base.
Stir in a scoop of plant protein after chilling (thin with milk if needed).
Top with hemp seeds or crushed roasted peanuts.
